name: EmotiVoice
tagline: Multi-voice, prompt-controlled text-to-speech with emotional synthesis
metaDescription: EmotiVoice is a free, open-source text-to-speech engine with over 2000 voices, English and Chinese support, and prompt-controlled emotional speech synthesis.
overview: EmotiVoice is a free, open-source text-to-speech engine from NetEase Youdao. It speaks both English and Chinese and offers more than 2000 different voices, so you can pick a sound that fits your project.

Its standout feature is emotional synthesis. By writing a short style prompt, you can make the same voice sound happy, excited, sad, angry, or many other moods. EmotiVoice ships with an easy web interface for trying voices and a scripting interface for generating speech in batches.
features: Over 2000 voices across English and Chinese
Emotional synthesis controlled by simple text prompts (happy, excited, sad, angry, and more)
Interactive Streamlit web demo page for quick experiments
Batch generation through a command-line scripting interface
An OpenAI-compatible TTS API for easy integration, including voice speed control
Voice cloning support using your own recorded data

gettingStarted.intro: The quickest way to try EmotiVoice is the Docker image, which needs a machine with an NVIDIA GPU. You can also do a full local install with conda for more control.

steps.heading: Run the Docker image
steps.body: Pull and start the official image, then open the web interface in your browser at http://localhost:8501. The OpenAI-compatible TTS API is available at http://localhost:8000/.

steps.heading: Full installation with conda
steps.body: Create a Python 3.8 environment and install the dependencies if you prefer a local setup instead of Docker.
steps.code:
conda create -n EmotiVoice python=3.8 -y
conda activate EmotiVoice
pip install torch torchaudio
pip install numpy numba scipy transformers soundfile yacs g2p_en jieba pypinyin pypinyin_dict
python -m nltk.downloader "averaged_perceptron_tagger_eng"

steps.heading: Launch the interactive demo page
steps.body: After installing the pretrained models, start the Streamlit demo to try voices and emotions in your browser.
steps.code:
pip install streamlit
streamlit run demo_page.py

steps.heading: Start the OpenAI-compatible API
steps.body: Install the API dependencies and run the server to call EmotiVoice from any OpenAI-style TTS client.
steps.code:
pip install fastapi pydub uvicorn[standard] pyrubberband
uvicorn openaiapi:app --reload

useCases: Adding expressive, emotion-aware narration to videos, games, or apps
Generating bilingual English and Chinese voiceovers at scale through batch scripts
Dropping a self-hosted TTS backend into existing tools via the OpenAI-compatible API
Cloning a personal voice from your own recordings for custom speech output
